Daily Report

14 June 2026

This report is updated automatically each day to highlight key insights from the MosquitoAlertES platform. It focuses on the 6-day-ahead forecast of the Vector Risk Index (VRI) for tiger mosquitoes (Aedes albopictus) in Spain. This provides a relative measure of the variation in the risk of human contact with tiger mosquitoes across Spain and over time. The 6-day-ahead forecast (which starts on the current day) is based on the weather forecast provided daily by the Spanish Meteorological Agency.

Country-Wide Trends

During the next 7 available days (based on data through June 14), we estimate the mean VRI in Spain will be 0.02 and the maximum will be 0.6, occuring on June 15 in Burlada/Burlata, Navarra, Comunidad Foral de Navarra. Figure 1 shows the mean and maximum values estimated for each day, starting 30 days before the latest available data and extending through the most recent forecasts.

Figure 1: Estimated country-wide VRI during past 30 days and coming 6 days. Red line indicates maximum values each day; blue indicates means. Vertical black dotted line shows latest available day.

CCAA Trends

Figure 2 shows the mean and maximum VRI estimates for each of Spain’s Autonomous Communities (CCAAs) from during the past 365 days (top panel) and for the week ahead (bottom panel).

Figure 2: Mean and maximum VRI estimates by CCAA during the past year (left) and in the week ahead (right).

The following sections show mean and maximum values for each autonomous community.

Andalucía

During the next 7 available days, we estimate the mean VRI in Andalucía will be 0.02, and the maximum will be 0.59, occuring on June 15 in Castilleja de la Cuesta, Sevilla. As a comparison, during the past 30 days, the mean VRI has been 0.02, and the maximum has been 0.55, occuring on May 15 in Castilleja de la Cuesta, Sevilla. During the past 365 days, the mean VRI has been 0.02, and the maximum has been 0.75, occuring on August 08 in Castilleja de la Cuesta, Sevilla.
